file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1114.lean` — Erdős Problem 1114

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` (line 17).
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–26).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 1114`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/1114](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1114)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (monotonicity of gaps between consecutive zeros of the derivative of a polynomial with roots in arithmetic progression).
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[Ba60b]` for Balint's proof.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1114 ... end Erdos1114` (lines 31–59).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1114`.
- [x] `open scoped BigOperators` and `open Polynomial Finset` appear **before** the namespace block (lines 28–29).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A — no helper `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 49).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1114` (snake_case, matching the namespace number).
- [x] Has `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 26]` (line 48).
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 33–47) describing the problem statement in mathematical detail.
- [x] Docstring references the source paper `[Ba60b]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is a direct assertion (prove monotonicity of gaps). This is **Case 3** — no `answer()` wrapper needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (existential statement about zeros with monotonicity), with no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] Quantifiers `(n : ℕ) (hn : 2 ≤ n) (a d : ℝ) (hd : 0 < d)` are theorem parameters, which is appropriate for Case 3 (no `answer()` involved).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute: `category research solved`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (solved but not formally verified).
- [x] Consistent with the proof containing `sorry` — `research solved` permits `sorry` in the proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 26`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `26` (real functions).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number — trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `26` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses multi-line `by` / `sorry` format (`:= by` on line 56, `sorry` indented on line 57). Acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches included for this solved problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. Single theorem only.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] Indentation consistent (2-space for proof body; continuation lines in type signature use appropriate deeper indentation).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used throughout: `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `∀`, `∃`, `∧`, `≤`, `↑`, `∈`, `∏`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run — cannot verify. Structure and syntax appear correct by inspection.

---

**Verdict:** All checkable items pass. The formalization is clean, well-documented, correctly categorized as `research solved` with `AMS 26`, and follows the style guidelines. The only unchecked item is build verification, which requires running `lake build`.

content.text: The review passed — all checkable items pass with the only unchecked item being build verification (which requires running `lake build`). No changes needed to `deepmind/1114.lean`.
